title: Moldova's president nominates pro-European businessman Vasile Tofan for prime minister
content: If approved by parliament, Tofan will be tasked with driving Moldova closer to realising its EU membership goal and reviving an economy that has experienced years-long decline.
snippet: If approved by parliament, Tofan will be tasked with driving Moldova closer to realising its EU membership goal and reviving an economy that has experienced years-long decline.

title: Eiffel Tower and other Paris landmarks to close early as another heatwave sweeps France
content: 24 departments across northwestern France are under a red heat alert on Saturday, according to Météo-France, affecting more than 22 million people.
snippet: 24 departments across northwestern France are under a red heat alert on Saturday, according to Météo-France, affecting more than 22 million people.

title: Police rescue cashier after 11-hour hostage standoff at Berlin supermarket
content: Staff members who were in the supermarket at the time of the incident were able to leave the building unharmed and are being attended to by emergency services.
snippet: Staff members who were in the supermarket at the time of the incident were able to leave the building unharmed and are being attended to by emergency services.

title: Russian missile strikes on Kyiv injure at least 11
content: According to the Ukrainian air force, Russia launched six Iskander ballistic missiles, four Kh-59/69 cruise missiles, two Kh-31 anti-radiation missiles, and 121 attack drones at Ukraine overnight.
snippet: According to the Ukrainian air force, Russia launched six Iskander ballistic missiles, four Kh-59/69 cruise missiles, two Kh-31 anti-radiation missiles, and 121 attack drones at Ukraine overnight.

title: Belgium to introduce new road tax in 2027 even for transiting drivers, regions say
content: According to the chairman of the liberal-conservative MR party, the government intends to offset the new toll by lowering other taxes for Belgians.

title: Ukrainian drone strikes hit southern Russian refineries and Azov port, officials say
content: Kyiv says the strikes are fair retribution for Russia's more than four-year barrages on Ukraine and that it is a bid to force Russia to the negotiating table.

title: Baltic states summon Russian envoys over false deportation claims
content: Lithuania and Estonia have said the Kremlin has been spreading false claims about deportations of Russian speakers from Baltic states in an attempt to distract from its large-scale attacks on cities and other civilian targets in Ukraine.

title: Inside the EU’s bittersweet deal to update air passenger rights
content: A decade-long battle over air passenger rights ended this week as the European Parliament approved the first overhaul in 22 years. The deal preserves existing rights and adds new ones, but consumer groups say it falls short, while airline associations argue there are no winners.

title: Man arrested on suspicion of murder after former MP Ann Widdecombe found dead, UK police say
content: Widdecombe served in the House of Commons as an MP from 1987 to 2010 and was known for socially conservative views opposing abortion rights and expansion of LGBTQ+ rights.
snippet: Widdecombe served in the House of Commons as an MP from 1987 to 2010 and was known for socially conservative views opposing abortion rights and expansion of LGBTQ+ rights.

title: Burnham on course to become next UK PM with 322 Labour MPs backing him in first vote
content: If Burnham reaches at least 323 nominations then it would no longer be mathematically possible for another challenger to get the 81 signatures required to join the race out of the total of 402 Labour MPs.

title: Is Belgium becoming a narco-state? Belgian politicians debate on The Ring

title: 'I'm not a candidate': Lagarde tells Euronews as French election speculation grows
content: Lagarde told Euronews that she is not "a candidate", while vowing to continue championing European ideals. Speculation that she could leave the ECB early to play an active role in the campaign is rife in Brussels and Paris.

title: 'Digital euro will not replace cash,' Lagarde tells Euronews as negotiations advance
content: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde rejected claims in an exclusive interview with Euronews that the digital euro is designed to replace cash or allow the central bank to monitor payments, as the legislation entered a new phase following months of talks in the European Parliament.

title: Exclusive: ‘Big mistake to believe we're off the hook in Europe and beyond,’ IEA tells Euronews

title: UK took 'unlawful' decision on migrants sent to France, High Court judge rules
content: As of early March, 377 people had been returned to France and 380 people had arrived in the UK under the "one in, one out" scheme, according to the Home Office.

title: French government activates new 'extreme heat' Orsec plan as temperatures rise
content: The Orsec plan is an emergency response and relief mechanism for exceptional events, and has generally been activated in the case of natural disasters such as floods.
snippet: The Orsec plan is an emergency response and relief mechanism for exceptional events, and has generally been activated in the case of natural disasters such as floods.

title: Ukraine's commander-in-chief says turning point in war with Russia 'still a long way off'
content: "The aggressor has not abandoned its plans for the complete occupation of the Luhansk and Donetsk regions," Oleksandr Syrskyi wrote in a post on Telegram.

title: Greek anti-terrorist police arrest three for deadly bombings targeting politicians
content: The 1 July dawn attacks in the northern city of Thessaloniki targeted the homes and vehicles of three politicians from Greece's ruling New Democracy party with homemade gas canister explosives.
snippet: The 1 July dawn attacks in the northern city of Thessaloniki targeted the homes and vehicles of three politicians from Greece's ruling New Democracy party with homemade gas canister explosives.

title: EU agrees to open new phase of membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova
content: Ukraine and Moldova will formally open cluster six of accession negotiations on 14 July. This leaves them with four clusters still blocked.
snippet: Ukraine and Moldova will formally open cluster six of accession negotiations on 14 July. This leaves them with four clusters still blocked.

title: Metropolitan Police investigating donations to hard-right Reform party, UK media say

title: Luxury paper made in Italy from waste : a way to reduce pressure on forests

title: Majority of 12 victims of Spain wildfires believed to be foreigners, officials say

title: Hungary to join European Prosecutor's Office, allowing probes into Orbán-era corruption allegations

title: French economy minister on Spain's debt proposal: 'It's interesting'
content: French Economy Minister Roland Lescure reacts to Spain's proposal for a new joint EU debt instrument worth up to €850 billion, while warning of potential risks. title: Portuguese rapper jailed in Germany for drug trafficking is deported to Portugal

title: Newly released footage shows revolver gifted by Erdogan to NATO leaders
content: Images shared by the Lithuanian Presidency show a revolver given by Turkey's Erdogan to every NATO leader as a parting gift after the summit in Ankara. title: Ukraine opens criminal probe after crowds attack army conscription vehicle in Lviv
content: Violence against recruitment officers was almost non-existent at the start of the war, but has proliferated in recent years as the fighting has dragged on and fatigue set in among the population.
